payload.source_text: High Level Description
The pocket money tracker system allows children to claim pocket money tasks as they are completed throughout the week.
Parents can detail tasks that should be completed and the monetary amount earned for that task.
As a task is completed it is linked to the child that completed it.
The children should be able to see the available tasks and the tasks they have completed that week.
At the end of each week the children can see the total amount they have earned that week.
The Parents can also see a report of how much each child has earned and the tasks they completed so that they can provide the pocket money earned to each child.
At the start of the next week all tasks become available again to be claimed.
Historical task data shall be available.
The resulting system shall be able to operate on iOS 16, Android 9 and Windows 11 or later.

At the start of the next week all tasks become available again to be claimed.

The resulting system shall be able to operate on iOS 16, Android 9 and Windows 11 or later.

payload.description: Child tracking of earned pocket money through task completion
payload.justification: Core strategic process enabling children to monitor their earnings.

payload.description: Parental definition of compensated task assignments
payload.justification: Strategic process by which parents establish the earning framework for children.

payload.description: Parental oversight of financial accountability and payment administration
payload.justification: Strategic process ensuring financial governance and transaction management.

payload.description: Longitudinal tracking for historical accountability
payload.justification: Strategic process enabling temporal analysis and accountability over extended periods.

payload.description: Pocket money as trackable financial asset earned by children
payload.justification: Core entity of strategic importance representing the financial value tracked and managed within the enterprise context.

payload.description: Task as compensable work unit assigned from parent to child
payload.justification: Fundamental entity representing the work assignments that drive the earning mechanism. | Distinct entity representing the relationship instance between parent, child, and task with compensation attributes.

payload.description: Payment transaction record for financial administration
payload.justification: Entity representing the financial transaction instances requiring parental oversight and administration.

payload.description: Historical record for longitudinal accountability tracking
payload.justification: Strategic entity enabling retrospective analysis and accountability over time.

payload.description: Task completion drives pocket money earning relationship
payload.justification: Core strategic relationship linking task execution to financial outcome.

payload.description: Weekly accountability and earning visibility cycle
payload.justification: Strategic temporal cycle defining the rhythm of accountability and earning visibility.

payload.description: Child as pocket money earner and tracker
payload.justification: Primary stakeholder role representing children who earn and monitor pocket money through task completion.

payload.description: Parent as task definer and financial administrator
payload.justification: Primary stakeholder role representing parents who define compensated tasks and oversee financial administration.

payload.description: Enable children to track earned pocket money through task completion
payload.justification: Core strategic purpose directly expressed as the enterprise objective for child engagement.

payload.description: Enable parents to define compensated task assignments for children
payload.justification: Strategic objective supporting parental capability to establish the earning framework.

payload.description: Provide child visibility and weekly accountability as motivational transparency for earning tracking
payload.justification: Strategic goal leveraging transparency to drive child motivation and engagement. | Strategic objective defining temporal rhythm for accountability mechanisms.

payload.description: Enable parental oversight for financial accountability and payment administration
payload.justification: Strategic goal ensuring financial governance and administrative control.

payload.description: Support longitudinal tracking and historical accountability
payload.justification: Strategic objective enabling retrospective analysis and extended accountability.

payload.name: Participant Roles and Accountability
payload.description: Defines who participates in the household economic arrangement, their respective accountabilities, and the fundamental relational structure governing child earning capacity and parental administrative authority.

payload.name: Financial Stewardship and Payment Obligation
payload.description: Establishes the enterprise commitment to administer compensable work arrangements, maintain payment records, and ensure parental oversight of financial obligations owed to child participants through completed work.

payload.name: Work Assignment and Compensation Governance
payload.description: Defines the enterprise governance structure for compensable work units, the relationship between task completion and earning entitlement, and the framework by which effort translates to financial recognition.

payload.name: Earning Transparency and Motivational Accountability
payload.description: Commits the enterprise to provide visibility into earning status, enable self-tracking by child participants, and maintain weekly accountability cycles that reinforce transparency as a motivational governance mechanism.

payload.name: Historical Accountability and Record Retention
payload.description: Establishes the enterprise obligation to maintain longitudinal records that support retrospective accountability, enable audit of past transactions, and preserve institutional memory of financial commitments over time.

payload.completeness_rule: This register SHALL contain the identifiers of ALL Domain elements present in the ledger.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall recognise children as participants entitled to earn and track pocket money.
payload.rationale: Establishes the fundamental participant role and entitlement of children within the household economic arrangement at enterprise scope.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall recognise parents as participants accountable for defining compensated task assignments and administering financial arrangements.
payload.rationale: Establishes the fundamental participant role and dual accountability (task definition and financial administration) of parents within the household economic arrangement at enterprise scope.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall enable parents to define compensated task assignments for children.
payload.rationale: Expresses the enterprise commitment to provide the capability for parental definition of compensated tasks, derived from the motivational goal and the process content describing task assignment.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall recognise pocket money as a compensable financial obligation earned by children through completed work.
payload.rationale: Establishes the enterprise commitment to recognise the earning relationship between task completion and pocket money as a financial obligation.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall maintain payment transaction records for financial administration.
payload.rationale: Obligates the enterprise to maintain records of payment transactions to support financial stewardship.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall ensure parental oversight of financial accountability and payment administration.
payload.rationale: Commits the enterprise to governance ensuring that parents exercise oversight over financial obligations and payments.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall recognise a task as a compensable work unit assigned from parent to child.
payload.rationale: Establishes the fundamental structural entity (task) and its enterprise-scope relationship properties (compensable, assigned parent-to-child).

payload.statement: The enterprise shall establish a relationship whereby task completion drives pocket money earning entitlement.
payload.rationale: Defines the governing rule that constrains the enterprise's compensation structure: completion triggers entitlement.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall enable child participants to track earned pocket money through task completion.
payload.rationale: Derived from both the process of child tracking (How) and the motivational goal of enabling self-tracking (Why). These CCIs express a single enterprise commitment to provide tracking capability to child participants.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall provide visibility to child participants regarding their earning status.
payload.rationale: Expresses the enterprise commitment to transparency by providing visibility of earnings to children as a motivational mechanism.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall maintain a weekly accountability cycle for earning visibility.
payload.rationale: Combines the temporal cycle constraint (When) with the motivational governance rule (Why) that establishes weekly accountability as a normative operational rhythm. The weekly cycle is a governance constraint on how accountability is maintained.
payload.fit_criteria: Accountability cycle repeats with seven-day periodicity

payload.statement: The enterprise shall maintain historical records that enable longitudinal accountability.
payload.rationale: All three CCIs converge on a single enterprise obligation: to maintain records over time for accountability purposes. The What-column CCI identifies the historical record entity, the How-column CCI describes longitudinal tracking, and the Why-column CCI expresses the underlying goal. This is a governance constraint on the enterprise to preserve institutional memory and enable retrospective audit, not a system function.
